mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and lower real-rate pressure on equities, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month when the policy rate is falling from an already restrictive level.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.25 pp over 2 months
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle after prior tightening
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of rate-cut episodes, or if the signal's rank correlation stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and deteriorating labor demand, which should reduce earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears once PAYEMS is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST reflects broad demand strength in consumption and housing, which supports revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform either series alone, or if positive 60-day SPX returns are not concentrated in periods when both series trend higher together.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly slowdown in INDPRO signals an industrial production contraction that should precede weaker corporate revenue and margin expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month over month by at least 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO shocks are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the hit rate falls below 55% in rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real rates are deeply negative and equities reprice upward as liquidity conditions stay easy over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above FEDFUNDS by at least 2.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with inflation above policy rate do not deliver above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes once FEDFUNDS is above CPI inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in GDPC1 growth indicates weakening aggregate demand and lower future earnings growth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP deceleration fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses directional accuracy when GDP revisions are accounted for.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS combined with a decline in UNRATE captures a genuine labor-market improvement that supports consumer spending and raises S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE improvement does not outperform either series alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns are not concentrated in labor recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST should lead broader demand stabilization and improved cyclical earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts expansions are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in recovery regimes, or if the signal is absent outside periods of sequential housing improvement.
